notes: Antigravity is a transcript-first adapter: agy creates the <conv-id> brain dir at launch but writes .system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl only after the first user input, so the conv-id-keyed session (SessionIDFromPath off the <conv-id> dir) is born in ready once the first turn's terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE (no tool_calls) is parsed, then cycles ready->working->ready as the turn replays. PID binding is best-effort and deliberately NOT asserted: it requires the parser to harvest a cwd from a run_command tool call's Cwd arg (parser.go:163), but agy reports its sandbox scratch dir (~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/scratch) as the Cwd rather than the launch cwd, so DiscoverPID never matches the agy process and the conv-id session never binds a PID. The daemon DOES mint a proc-<pid> presession (live-confirmed: presession_created proc-56210 at the staging cwd, seq 125 of the 2026-06-20 list_dir recording) but never re-binds it to the conv-id, so no pid_discovered carries the conv-id and the curator cannot link the presession to the session row. PID=0 sessions are first-class for this adapter by design (agent.go:25). Observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage (tokens live in the per-conversation SQLite protobuf), so the parser surfaces none. Model is harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>: live recordings on this account consistently report 'Gemini 3.1 Pro' (the settings line reads 'Model Selection from None to Gemini 3.1 Pro (Low)'), so the observed model is 3.1 Pro, not the 3.5 Flash default banner.

text: Conv-id-keyed session appears in ready after the first turn (when agy writes the transcript)
The first turn drives the session ready->working on the first transcript activity
The terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls settles the session back to ready
